title: oxydectes clavuligera
intro: Oxydectes clavuligera is a plant species commonly known as the "tongue-axe," which belongs to the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and fever. The plant is believed to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving digestive discomfort, and potentially lowering fever. Herbal preparations of Oxydectes clavuligera often include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
